tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent rebounded to $99.99/bbl on Mar 12 after the Mar 11 IEA and US reserve release failed to normalise shipping risk. The strikes on two fuel-oil tankers near al-Faw and renewed Basra disruption confirm the shock is no longer confined to Hormuz. Hold core long exposure and add on pullbacks into the low $90s rather than chasing vertical moves. Near-term working range is $98-118; stress case remains $120+. PTTEP and Medco remain the cleanest ASEAN equity beta.

tradeIdeas.title: Rebuild ASEAN Energy Long / Underweight ASEAN Banks
tradeIdeas.rationale: The Mar 10 de-escalation reversal has faded. Brent is back near $100, ASEAN Energy Equities proxy is 145.1 (+4.8%), and ASEAN Banks proxy slipped to 198.7 (-2.4%) as higher fuel costs re-price inflation and subsidy risk. Rebuild energy exposure on producers and integrated names; fade rate-sensitive domestic banks that lose the most if oil stays near triple digits. Keep the position paired rather than outright because reserve-release headlines can still trigger violent intraday squeezes.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short — Elevated Conviction on Renewed Oil Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP rose to 59.67 on Mar 12 (+0.7% day-on-day) as Brent returned to $99.99 and the tanker attacks near Iraq pushed fuel-security risk back into focus. Philippines import sensitivity reasserts quickly once crude approaches $100. Keep the short-PHP bias; spot is again moving toward the 60.00 handle and local fuel-inflation optics worsen materially if Brent trades through $105. Retain a 61.00 stress target and keep stop discipline below 58.50 if a credible ceasefire regime finally appears.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short — Oil Import Stress Back in Play
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/IDR printed 16,908 on Mar 12 while Indonesia 10Y held near 6.69%, a combination that keeps Bank Indonesia on intervention watch rather than easing mode. The reserve release capped panic but did not restore shipping confidence, and Indonesia remains exposed if Brent spends another week near $100. Maintain the short-IDR bias, especially against renewed tanker or export-terminal disruption. Near-term stress target remains 17,200-17,500 with a tail scenario back above 18,000 if the maritime war broadens.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection — Hold Position
tradeIdeas.rationale: The iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG proxy moved back out to 156bp on Mar 12 (+4bp) and the ASEAN HY composite widened to 498bp (+18bp) as renewed ship attacks offset the reserve-release relief. Hold protection. The market has proved it will not re-tighten sustainably until merchant traffic is actually protected, not just politically discussed. If Brent breaks and holds above $105, 165-180bp remains credible; trim only if escorted flows normalise and oil re-prices below $90 for several sessions.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF — Keep Optionality
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM last confirmed at $15.98/MMBtu on Mar 11 versus TTF at €49.22/MWh the same session. The reserve release helps crude faster than LNG, while Asia still faces Qatar restart uncertainty and freight risk through Hormuz. Keep a smaller JKM-over-TTF relative long for optionality rather than sizing it as the core book. If escorted LNG transits improve materially, cut exposure quickly; if outages spread beyond Hormuz, JKM can catch up sharply from here.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy's 2026-2027 refinancing problem is back in the foreground. Brent at $99.99 supports upstream capex eventually, but iTraxx Asia IG at 156bp and the ASEAN HY composite at 498bp keep unsecured funding effectively shut. Push immediately toward secured bank lines, private credit, and DFI-backed structures. Do not frame the Mar 11 reserve release as a reopening window; shipping risk and oil volatility are still too high for a clean HY re-entry.

PTTEP should still pre-fund while Thailand 10Y remains near 1.93% and THB is only modestly weaker at 31.92. Brent back at $99.99 materially improves upstream EBITDA, but another leg higher in oil would pull Thai inflation expectations up again and can close the local-duration window quickly. Execute the H2 2026 prefunding plan now and run a sanctions/compliance review on any Gulf-exposed JV structure in parallel.

Expand USD revolving-credit capacity now. At Brent $99.99, Wilmar's bunker and feedstock burden is back near the top of the March stress range even with USD/SGD only at 1.2762. The Mar 11 reserve release reduced panic but not shipping disruption. Use the still-functional IG bank market to extend tenor and add committed liquidity before another jump in freight or sanctions screening tightens trade-finance appetite.

Hyflux should be back on an urgent restructuring track. Base probability is down to 20% while the stress case is 56%, Brent is back near $100, and ASEAN HY is 498bp. That combination keeps the unsecured market effectively closed and worsens power-cost pressure on desalination assets. Engage private-credit and quasi-sovereign options now; do not wait for a cleaner market window that may not arrive if Gulf shipping keeps deteriorating.
